rationale: Both candidates catch the central trust_remote_code default-flip bug (GT's top inline concern) with correct grounding, and both implicitly verify the deploy-override/endpoint-restriction precedence fix is now correct. Y edges ahead by catching a verified dead-code bug (hf_config=None always returned) that's directly visible in the diff, and by flagging that Qwen2.5-Omni's identical thinker-talker architecture likely needs the same completions restriction — a concrete, specific echo of the PR discussion's explicit ask to 'audit other models that can be crashed,' which X never mentions. X's review is well-organized (blocker-scan table, verdict) but its extra findings (error-response fallback pattern, else-branch skipping UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES) are more speculative and don't map to any GT concern, giving it a similar precision but slightly lower recall than Y.
